es.question: Teniendo en cuenta que el plato divisor de la A218 se controla como eje C, ¿qué secuencia de teclas se debe pulsar en el teclado del CNC 8070 para realizar una búsqueda de referencia manual exclusiva para este eje?
es.answer: To home the rotary table, select the C axis on the alphanumeric keyboard, press the ZERO key, and then press START to execute the movement.

es.question: Si un operario está ejecutando un ciclo automático en la brochadora A218 y necesita detener la máquina para inspeccionar visualmente la brocha con las puertas abiertas, ¿qué secuencia de acciones debe realizar combinando el panel de operador y el menú del CNC?
es.answer: The operator must press the STOP key on the operator panel to interrupt the program, access tool inspection from the CNC's vertical softkey menu, enable the Set-up selector on the panel, and press the 'Apertura puertas' button to open the doors.

sample_rows: In general, all the changes made in MDI/MDA mode are kept active when resuming the program after tool inspection except the following functions that are restored at the time of interruption; type of interpolation (G00, G01, G02, G03, G33 or G63), G90/G91 function or #MCS function.
Tool inspection may be accessed from the vertical softkey menu only when the execution of the program has been interrupted ([STOP] key).
After activating tool inspection, it is possible to jog the axes using the jog keyboard, act upon the master spindle of the channel from the operator panel and execute blocks from the MDI/MDA mode.
When switching to execution mode (after pressing the softkey), the CNC goes into tool inspection to reposition the axes, modify program conditions, etc.

The conditions when entering the MDI/MDA mode will be those of the interruption point; i.e. the CNC maintains the history of active G and M functions, feedrate, spindle speed, tool and other commands that were programmed.

es.question: Para verificar la sintaxis y trayectoria de un programa de brochado en la A218 sin que se muevan físicamente el carro porta-piezas ni se activen las funciones M en el PLC, ¿qué tipo de simulación se debe utilizar en el CNC 8070?
es.answer: Theoretical travel simulation must be selected in the EDISIMU mode, as it simulates the programmed tool path without moving the axes or sending M-H-S-T controls to the PLC.
es.normalized_question: para verificar la sintaxis y trayectoria de un programa de brochado en la a218 sin que se muevan fisicamente el carro porta-piezas ni se activen las funciones m en el plc que tipo de simulacion se debe utilizar en el cnc 8070

sample_rows: Select a program. The "Open program" softkey is used to select a program in EDISIMU mode and may be a new program or an existing one. A different program may be edited and executed in each channel. When selecting this option, the CNC shows a list of the available programs. 1 Select the folder that contains the program. If it is a new program, it will be saved in this folder. 2 Select the program from the list or write its name in the bottom window. To edit a new program, write the name of the program in the lower window and the CNC will open an empty program or a predefined template depending on how the editor is configured. 3 Press [ENTER] to accept the selection and open the program or [ESC] to cancel it and close the program listing. Simulating a program. 1 Choose the type of graphic representation, its dimensions and the point of view. This data may also be modified during the simulation of the program. 2 Activate the desired simulation options using the icon menu. 3 Press the START icon to start the simulation. The simulation may be interrupted with the STOP icon or canceled with the RESET icon. The simulation of the program starts at the first block of the program and ends after executing one of the end-of-program functions "M02" or "M30". As an option, it is possible to define the first and last blocks of the simulation. To simulate the program, the CNC assumes the real configuration of the spindles of the channel and the configuration of the machine parameters. “ Single block” simulation. The program may be simulated in –single block– or –automatic– mode; the mode may be selected even while simulating the program. Simulation options.
With simulated execution, it is possible to simulate a program, interrupt it at a point and start execution from that point on.

evidence_candidates.subject: EDISIMU_MODE
evidence_candidates.obj: Select a program. The "Open program" softkey is used to select a program in EDISIMU mode and may be a new program or an existing one. A different program may be edited and executed in each channel. When selecting this option, the CNC shows a list of the available programs. 1 Select the folder that contains the program. If it is a new program, it will be saved in this folder. 2 Select the program from the list or write its name in the bottom window. To edit a new program, write the name of the program in the lower window and the CNC will open an empty program or a predefined template depending on how the editor is configured. 3 Press [ENTER] to accept the selection and open the program or [ESC] to cancel it and close the program listing. Simulating a program. 1 Choose the type of graphic representation, its dimensions and the point of view. This data may also be modified during the simulation of the program. 2 Activate the desired simulation options using the icon menu. 3 Press the START icon to start the simulation. The simulation may be interrupted with the STOP icon or canceled with the RESET icon. The simulation of the program starts at the first block of the program and ends after executing one of the end-of-program functions "M02" or "M30". As an option, it is possible to define the first and last blocks of the simulation. To simulate the program, the CNC assumes the real configuration of the spindles of the channel and the configuration of the machine parameters. “ Single block” simulation. The program may be simulated in –single block– or –automatic– mode; the mode may be selected even while simulating the program. Simulation options.

es.question: Si se pulsa una seta de emergencia en la A218 interrumpiendo un ciclo de mecanizado, al restaurar el sistema y utilizar la búsqueda de bloque automática en el CNC, ¿es necesario introducir manualmente el número de bloque de parada?
es.answer: No, it is not necessary. In the automatic block search, the CNC runs the search up to the block where the program was interrupted, even if the machine was stopped due to an emergency.
es.normalized_question: si se pulsa una seta de emergencia en la a218 interrumpiendo un ciclo de mecanizado al restaurar el sistema y utilizar la busqueda de bloque automatica en el cnc es necesario introducir manualmente el numero de bloque de parada

trace.query_text: SELECT DISTINCT ?seed WHERE { VALUES ?seed { <https://vocab.cfaa.eus/broaching/BusquedaBloqueAutomatica> <https://vocab.cfaa.eus/broaching/BusquedaBloque> } }

sample_rows: The automatic block search may be used to recover the program history up to the block where the previous execution was canceled. The CNC remembers the block where interruption was canceled, thus not being necessary to set the stop block.

Executing block search. 1 Selecting the type of search: automatic or manual. 2 Selecting the stop block. In the automatic block search, there is no need to select the stop block; by default, the CNC runs the search up to the block where the program was interrupted. 3 Selecting the starting block for the search. If the first block is not selected, the block search starts at the beginning of the program. 4 Press the [CYCLE START] key to start the block search. 5 Depending on how the treatment of functions M, H, F, S is configured, it may be necessary to decide which ones are sent out to the PLC. 6 Reposition the axes to the point to resume execution. 7 Tool inspection may be accessed to change the machining conditions. 8 Press [START] to execute the program.

es.question: Considerando que los parámetros de brochado de la A218 se guardan como programas en C:\FAGORCNC\Users\PRG, ¿cómo se puede evitar que un operador modifique estos ciclos desde el modo EDISIMU?
es.answer: The operator must go to the Utilities mode, access the folder C:\FAGORCNC\Users\PRG, select the program, and change the 'modifiable' attribute (indicated by the letter -M-).

sample_rows: Change the "modifiable" attribute of the selected files. The attributes column shows the letter -M- indicating that the program may be modified. This attribute is used to protect the files so they can't be modified from the EDISIMU mode.

Select a program. The "Open program" softkey is used to select a program in EDISIMU mode and may be a new program or an existing one. A different program may be edited and executed in each channel. When selecting this option, the CNC shows a list of the available programs. 1 Select the folder that contains the program. If it is a new program, it will be saved in this folder. 2 Select the program from the list or write its name in the bottom window. To edit a new program, write the name of the program in the lower window and the CNC will open an empty program or a predefined template depending on how the editor is configured. 3 Press [ENTER] to accept the selection and open the program or [ESC] to cancel it and close the program listing. Simulating a program. 1 Choose the type of graphic representation, its dimensions and the point of view. This data may also be modified during the simulation of the program. 2 Activate the desired simulation options using the icon menu. 3 Press the START icon to start the simulation. The simulation may be interrupted with the STOP icon or canceled with the RESET icon. The simulation of the program starts at the first block of the program and ends after executing one of the end-of-program functions "M02" or "M30". As an option, it is possible to define the first and last blocks of the simulation. To simulate the program, the CNC assumes the real configuration of the spindles of the channel and the configuration of the machine parameters. “ Single block” simulation. The program may be simulated in –single block– or –automatic– mode; the mode may be selected even while simulating the program. Simulation options.

es.answer: The CNC maintains the history of the active G and M functions, feedrate, and spindle speed from the point where the automatic cycle was interrupted.

es.question: Si se mecanizan múltiples piezas en diferentes posiciones del plato divisor de la A218, ¿qué códigos G del CNC 8070 permiten activar los decalajes de cero absolutos del 1 al 6?

es.question: Usando los atajos de teclado del panel FAGOR, ¿qué combinación abre directamente el modo Utilidades para poder borrar un programa de brochado de la A218?
es.answer: Press the key combination [CTRL] + [F12] to open the Utilities mode, and use the 'Delete' softkey to erase the program.

evidence_candidates.obj: Operations at the interface. Minimize/Maximize the CNC. Show / hide the virtual operator panel. Show / hide the PLC message list. Show / hide the CNC message list. Show / hide the window for errors and warnings. Turn the CNC off. Work modes. To show the task window. MAIN MENU Main menu. AUTO Automatic mode. MANUAL Jog mode. EDIT EDISIMU mode. MDI MDI/MDA mode. [ CTRL] + [F10] TABLES User tables. [ CTRL] + [F11] TOOLS Tool and magazine table. [ CTRL] + [F12] UTILITIES Utilities mode. CALC Calculator. Browsing keys. [ CTRL] + [F1] BACK Previous menu. [ CTRL] + [F2] FOCUS Switch window. [ CTRL] + [F3] NEXT Switch screens. Two-colored key. Execution keys. [ CTRL]+[S] Cycle start key (START). [ CTRL]+[P] Cycle stop key (STOP). [ CTRL]+[R] RESET Reset key. SINGLE Single-block execution mode. The shortcuts for the [START] [STOP] and [RESET] keys are only available when the CNC is installed as simulator on a PC.
